channel.title: GS MENA Conflict News Monitor
channel.description: Aggregated RSS from multiple Middle East sources.

item.title: Blanket evacuation orders in Lebanon raise international law concerns, says UN human rights chief
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Olivia Le Poidevin

GENEVA, March 6 (Reuters) - Large-scale evacuation orders issued by the Israeli army for southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs raise serious concerns under international law, the U.N. human rights chief said on Friday.

"These blanket, massive displacement orders we are talking here about hundreds and thousands of people," said United Nations human rights chief, Volker Turk.

"This raises serious concern under international humanitarian law, and in particular when it comes to issues around forced transfer," he added.

item.title: UAE blocking Algerians from evacuation flight: Report
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

UAE blocking Algerians from evacuation flight: Report

Authorities in the United Arab Emirates prevented Algerian nationals from boarding an evacuation flight as regional tensions escalated, according to the Algerian daily El Khabar

The newspaper said Emirati officials stopped members of Algeria’s community from boarding a flight operated by Air Algéerie as foreign nationals sought to leave Gulf countries amid ongoing Israeli-US war on Iran.

The report described the move as an “unjustified hostile act”, coming at a time when relations between Algiers and Abu Dhabi have already been strained in recent years.

“What the Emirati authorities have done is a totally irresponsible act which contravenes the requirements of international law and the norms of consular cooperation in times of international crises and conflicts,” said Hicham Benhaddad, a former rapporteur of the foreign affairs committee of Algeria’s National People’s Assembly.

“Emergency evacuations are considered urgent humanitarian measures based on established principles of international law, including the principle of protection of civilians and the principle of separation of humanitarian issues and political conflicts,” he added.

Algeria and the UAE have seen relations deteriorate in recent years. In February, Algiers cancelled a bilateral air services agreement that had governed flights between the two countries since 2013.

item.title: Epstein files with claims against Trump released by US Justice Department
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Files summarise a woman's accusations against Trump over alleged sexual encounter when she was aged between 13 and 15.

item.title: Iran war chokes aid corridors, obstructing global relief efforts
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Olivia Le Poidevin and Emma Farge

GENEVA, March 6 (Reuters) - Key humanitarian air, sea and land routes are being constricted by disruption from the war in the Middle East, delaying life-saving shipments to some of the world's worst crises, 10 aid officials have told Reuters.

The U.S.–Israeli war on Iran entered its seventh day on Friday, convulsing global markets and disrupting supply chains with airspace closures and the halt of shipping through the critical Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli air strike hits southern Lebanon
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Israeli air strike hits southern Lebanon

An Israeli air strike has targeted the southern Lebanese city of Sidon, Al Jazeera Arabic reported.

The strike hit the coastal city in southern Lebanon as Israeli attacks continue across several parts of the country.

item.title: Hezbollah Issues warning to residents of 23 northern Israeli settlements to evacuate after Beirut strikes
item.description:
[Middle East Monitor]
Hezbollah has warned residents of 23 northern Israeli settlements near the border to evacuate immediately, in response to what it described as Israeli attacks on Beirut and other areas of Lebanon.  In a statement, the group called on people living within 5km of the Lebanese border to leave at once. It said the warning was a response to “the Israeli aggression against Lebanon, civilians and infrastructure”.  In a statement, written in a style similar to Israeli military warnings, the group said: “Warning to residents of the northern settlements. You are required to evacuate all settlements located within 5km of the border line. The aggression of your army against Lebanese sovereignty, the targeting of peaceful civilians, the destruction of civilian infrastructure […]

item.title: Beit Shemesh family clings to God after 3 kids killed by Iran: ‘Faith isn’t built in a day’
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Sitting shiva in a Jerusalem hotel after her home was destroyed, Tamar Biton lights up as she describes Yaakov, Avigail and Sarah, among 9 killed in deadliest Iranian strike

The post Beit Shemesh family clings to God after 3 kids killed by Iran: ‘Faith isn’t built in a day’ appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Is BRICS bloc divided over US-Israel attacks on Iran?
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Since India took over as chair, the normally outspoken BRICS grouping appears to be more cautious.

item.title: Will the Houthis join Iran’s war?
item.description: [Al Jazeera] As the regional conflict escalates, Abdel-Malik al-Houthi faces a strategic choice: intervene or stay out.

item.title: Crew rescued after container ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz
item.description:
[Middle East Monitor]
A container ship managed by a UAE company was hit by a projectile while passing through the Strait of Hormuz, causing a fire in the engine room, before the Omani navy intervened to rescue its crew, including 21 Egyptians. Specialised maritime websites, including Marine Insight, Lloyd’s List and PortNews, reported that the Malta-flagged container vessel SAFEEN PRESTIGE was struck by a projectile while sailing in the Strait of Hormuz near the Omani coast on Wednesday evening. According to the reports, the projectile hit the rear section of the vessel above the waterline, sparking a fire inside the engine room and damaging the ship. The crew then issued a distress call and abandoned the vessel as a precaution. There were 24 […]

item.title: US-Israeli strikes kill 20 in Iran’s Shiraz province
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

US-Israeli strikes kill 20 in Iran’s Shiraz province

An Iranian provincial official said recent US and Israeli strikes have killed at least 20 people and wounded 30 in the southern city of Shiraz.

Jalil Hasani said the attack struck a residential area in the city’s Zibashahr district, according to Iranian state media.

He said the strike killed innocent citizens. Earlier reports also indicated that two paramedics were killed in a separate attack in Shiraz.

item.title: Iranian drones strike Amazon data centres in Bahrain, UAE
item.description:
[Middle East Monitor]
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has carried out a drone strike on an Amazon data centre in Bahrain, according to Iran’s state news agency Fars. Fars reported that the attack targeted an Amazon facility in Bahrain to identify how such centres support the military and intelligence activities of Tehran’s adversaries. The agency added that the strike was part of a series of recent IRGC operations against Amazon data centres in Dubai and other strategic sites across the region. Meanwhile, Amazon Web Services (AWS), the company’s cloud computing division, said last Monday, “In the UAE, two of our facilities were directly struck, while in Bahrain, a drone strike in close proximity to one of our facilities caused physical impacts to […]

item.title: Pakistani, Afghan border forces clash as UN says war displaces 100,000
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Mohammad Yunus Yawar

KABUL, March 6 (Reuters) - Pakistani and Afghan troops exchanged fire at dozens of points along their border on Friday as the U.N. said their week-old conflict has forced the displacement of more than 100,000 people.

The South Asian nations show no signs of rapprochement in their worst fighting in years, adding to the volatility in a region also contending with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran - a nation that borders both Afghanistan and Pakistan.

item.title: Extensive destruction in Beirut's southern suburbs following Israeli strikes
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

Israeli air strikes left behind massive destruction in Beirut's southern suburbs on Friday morning, with the Israeli military saying it had destroyed Hezbollah targets.

The attacks came after the Israeli military issued an unprecedented evacuation warning for the area, which is home to hundreds of thousands of people.

Rubble and dust covered a main road in one neighbourhood, while the apartment buildings surrounding it were heavily damaged, AFPTV footage showed.

The Lebanese health ministry said late on Thursday night that strikes since Monday had killed 123 people.

item.title: Does Mark Carney know where he stands on the US-Israeli war on Iran?
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Does Mark Carney know where he stands on the US-Israeli war on Iran?

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has spent almost as much time abroad as he has at home since his election last year, reaffirming and rebuilding his country's international relationships as the US becomes less of a reliable partner. 

On 28 February, while on a high-stakes visit to India as the US and Israel launched their war on Iran, Carney released a statement that made his position unabashedly clear.

After four paragraphs, in which he outlined why Iran provoked the attack, he said: "Canada supports the United States acting to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon and to prevent its regime from further threatening international peace and security."

The Trump administration, in fact, had not even fully settled on the nuclear issue as the key rationale for starting the war. But it seemed that Carney had. 

This is despite the US not obtaining a United Nations mandate for its mission, which has usually set the trajectory for Canadian foreign policy. 

Read more: Does Mark Carney know where he stands on the US-Israeli war on Iran?

item.title: Cost to US for war on Iran is $3.7bn in first 100 hours, says think tank
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Most of the costs are not budgeted for, meaning the Pentagon will need to request more funding soon, researchers say.

item.title: Tens of thousands in scramble for flights out of Middle East amid Iran war
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Wealthy travellers are paying up to $200,000 to charter flights out of the region.

item.title: Iran says it downed Israeli drone over Isfahan
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Iran says it downed Israeli drone over Isfahan

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has shot down an Israeli drone over the central city of Isfahan, according to Iranian media.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said its forces intercepted an Israeli Heron unmanned aircraft flying above the city, the Tehran Times reported.

The Heron is one of Israel’s largest unmanned aerial vehicles.

item.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, likely candidate to replace father as Iran’s supreme leader?
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Described as 'the power behind the robes,' son of slain ayatollah was said to serve as 'principal gatekeeper' to his late father; secretive figure has never been elected

The post Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, likely candidate to replace father as Iran’s supreme leader? appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Why did Trump fire Kristi Noem as DHS secretary; who is Markwayne Mullin?
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Trump said Mullin will lead the Department of Homeland Security while Noem assigned as special envoy.

item.title: At least 1,300 civilians killed in US-Israeli strikes since war on Iran began
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

At least 1,300 civilians killed in US-Israeli strikes since war on Iran began

At least 1,332 civilians have been killed since the start of the US-Israeli war on the country, the Iranian Red Crescent Society reported on Friday.

item.title: Anger in Cyprus over UK bases as US-Israel war with Iran endangers island
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Protests grow as attack on British military site stirs fears that Cyprus is being dragged into a regional conflict.

item.title: South Korea, US militaries discuss moving Patriot missiles to Iran war, Seoul says
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

SEOUL, March 6 (Reuters) - South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun said on Friday the U.S. and South Korean militaries are discussing the possible redeployment of some U.S. Patriot missile defence systems based in South Korea to be used in the war against Iran.

Cho was responding to questions at a parliamentary hearing following media reports on Friday that units of the U.S. mobile missile interceptor system had been moved to the Osan Air Base in South Korea from other locations in the country.

item.title: 52 French ships stuck in the Gulf amid Iran conflict, minister says
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

PARIS, March 6 (Reuters) - Around 50 French ships are currently blocked in the Persian Gulf and another eight in the Red Sea, France's Transport Minister Philippe Tabarot said on Friday, as Paris seeks support to build a coalition of countries to secure shipping traffic in the region.

"There are around fifty ships - 52 to be precise - in the Persian Gulf and eight in the Red Sea, and we are also in permanent contact with the crews, as there are French sailors aboard a number of these vessels," Tabarot told French broadcaster CNews/Europe 1.

item.title: Maersk suspends two shipping services due to Middle East crisis
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

COPENHAGEN, March 6 (Reuters) - Maersk, one of the world's biggest container shipping groups, said on Friday it has temporarily suspended two services linking the Middle East to Asia and Europe as the Iran conflict continues to disrupt global supply chains.

The Danish group said in a statement it would halt its FM1 service connecting the Far East to the Middle East and its ME11 service linking the Middle East to Europe

item.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

Here are the latest main events related to the war in the Middle East:

- 'Humanitarian disaster' -

Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam warned that "a humanitarian disaster is looming" as a result of Israeli evacuation orders that are causing a massive displacement of the population.

Residents of the southern suburbs of Beirut and of the south of the country, both Hezbollah strongholds, have fled in large numbers following unprecedentedly wide evacuation orders from the Israeli military.

- 'Transparent and impartial' -

item.title: Thousands displaced in Beirut after Israeli forced evacuation order
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Thousands displaced in Beirut after Israeli forced evacuation order

item.title: Most Asian equities drop as Mideast crisis rages, though oil dips
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

Asian markets mostly down Friday as the war in the Middle East showed no sign of ending, though there was some reprieve from the surge in oil prices after the United States looked to ease supply concerns.

After a torrid week on trading floors, investors were limping into the weekend wondering when the US-Israel war on Iran, and Tehran's attacks across the Gulf region, will come to an end.

item.title: Video: Israeli leader calls for depopulated 'sterile zones' in Lebanon
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Video: Israeli leader calls for depopulated 'sterile zones' in Lebanon

Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid said villages in southern Lebanon should be cleared to create what he described as a “sterile zone” along the border.

Speaking to Israeli channel i24 News, Lapid said it “may be unpleasant to scrape away two or three Lebanese villages,” but argued the measure was necessary. 

Lapid compared the proposed buffer zone to the so-called “Yellow Line” in Gaza, which separates Israeli forces from Palestinian areas.

In an interview with Israeli channel i24 News, Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid said that villages in southern Lebanon should be depopulated to create a “sterile zone” similar to the “Yellow Line” in Gaza pic.twitter.com/V2C4ZdYWk6

— Middle East Eye (@MiddleEastEye) March 6, 2026

item.title: More than 120 killed in Israel’s Lebanon attacks as Beirut, south, east hit
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Lebanon's Hezbollah group urges Israelis to evacuate border areas as Israel continues to bomb the country.

item.title: Indonesian president says he’ll bolt Board of Peace if it doesn’t help Palestinians
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Prabowo Subianto makes comments in meeting where he sought to reassure local Islamic critics of his decision to join the US-led board, whose activities are on hold due to Iran war

The post Indonesian president says he’ll bolt Board of Peace if it doesn’t help Palestinians appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: US-Israeli strikes killed paramedics in Shiraz attack, Iranian media say
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

US-Israeli strikes killed paramedics in Shiraz attack, Iranian media say

Two paramedics were killed in the Iranian city of Shiraz as US and Israeli attacks continue, according to Iran’s Tasnim news agency.

The report said the victims were emergency responders working at a Shiraz Emergency Medical Services base.

The kills come amid US and Israeli forces using “double-tap” strikes, in which a second attack targets the same location after rescuers arrive.

item.title: Wealthy Asians look to move Dubai assets closer to home on Iran war fears
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Xinghui Kok, Rae Wee and Yantoultra Ngui

SINGAPORE/DUBAI, March 6 (Reuters) - Soon after the first Iranian missile and drone attacks on Dubai last week, two Indian entrepreneurs based there tried to move more than $100,000 each from their local bank accounts to Singapore to hedge risk.

Technological glitches in the aftermath of the Iranian attacks initially scuppered those plans, the entrepreneurs, who did not wish to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter, told Reuters.

item.title: Israeli airstrikes pound Beirut suburb, Hezbollah warns Israelis
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

BEIRUT/JERUSALEM, March 6 (Reuters) - Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut overnight after ordering its residents to leave, while the Iran-backed group warned Israelis to leave towns and villages at the frontier.

Explosions and flashes lit up the night sky over the Beirut southern suburbs, Reuters footage showed. The Israeli military said it had carried out 26 waves of strikes overnight in the southern suburbs, saying targets included Hezbollah's command centres and weapons storage facilities.

item.title: US spent $3.7bn in first 100 hours of Iran war as costs mount, think tank says
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

US spent $3.7bn in first 100 hours of Iran war as costs mount, think tank says

The first 100 hours of the US war on Iran are estimated to have cost about $3.7 billion, or roughly $891.4 million a day, according to the Washington-based Centre for Strategic and International Studies.

The think tank said the early phase of the conflict is particularly expensive because it relies on advanced and costly munitions.

With President Donald Trump and Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth indicating the war could last for weeks, the report warned the Pentagon may need additional funding beyond its current budget.

item.title: Sudanese army retakes Bara, secures el-Obeid in North Kordofan
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Source tells Al Jazeera army destroyed 32 RSF combat vehicles, killed dozens of RSF fighters in clashes, drone attacks.

item.title: Video: Azerbaijan President calls drone strike on airport ‘act of terror’
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Video: Azerbaijan President calls drone strike on airport ‘act of terror’

Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev condemned a suspected Iranian drone strike on an airport as an “act of terror.”

The attack, which occurred on Thursday, left four people wounded, according to Azerbaijani authorities.

Aliyev said the strike targeted civilian infrastructure and called it a serious escalation amid the widening regional conflict.

Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev denounced a suspected Iranian drone strike on an airport on Thursday as an “act of terror” that left four people injured pic.twitter.com/i6KR2CjXJN

— Middle East Eye (@MiddleEastEye) March 6, 2026

item.title: Reports say Tehran military academy struck
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Reports say Tehran military academy struck

A military academy belonging to Iran’s army was reportedly among the sites hit in strikes in central Tehran on Friday.

Iranian media said the facility was struck as attacks continued across the capital.

The strike occurred while a reporter from Iran’s state broadcaster was reporting live near the location, according to Al Jazeera.

item.title: Iran says over 3,000 homes damaged in US-Israel attacks
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Iran says over 3,000 homes damaged in US-Israel attacks

Iran’s Red Crescent on Friday said more than 3,000 homes have been damaged in US and Israeli attacks across the country.

The organisation’s president, Pir Hossein Kolivand, said a total of 3,643 civilian sites were affected.

Kolivand said the damage includes 3,090 homes, 528 commercial and service centres, 14 medical or pharmaceutical facilities and nine Red Crescent sites. He added that many of the strikes hit densely populated residential areas.

item.title: Iran Red Crescent says 14 medical centres damaged in attacks
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Iran Red Crescent says 14 medical centres damaged in attacks

The Iranian Red Crescent said 14 medical centres and nine of its affiliated facilities were damaged during recent attacks by Israel and the United States, Al Jazeera Arabic reported.

The organisation said the damage occurred as strikes intensified across several parts of Iran.

In a statement cited by Iranian media, the Red Crescent said the facilities were hit or affected during the bombardment, but it did not immediately provide details on casualties or the exact locations of the damaged centres.

item.title: Israeli researchers find cannabis compounds could lead to 1st drug for fatty liver disease
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

As 1 out of every 3 adults worldwide suffers from the condition, Hebrew University scientists use non-psychoactive substances to change how the organ manages energy and cleans itself

The post Israeli researchers find cannabis compounds could lead to 1st drug for fatty liver disease appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Tehran hit by heavy bombing on day seven of US-Israel war on Iran
item.description: [Al Jazeera] An Al Jazeera correspondent in Tehran says more intense bombardment is also targeting civilian and residential areas.

item.title: Israel says it launched 26 waves of strikes in Beirut suburbs
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Israel says it launched 26 waves of strikes in Beirut suburbs

Israel’s military says it has carried out 26 waves of attacks targeting Beirut’s southern suburbs since the war began.

In a statement, the Israel Defense Forces said its forces struck several sites overnight.

The army said the raids targeted headquarters and 10 high-rise buildings that it claimed were "containing Hezbollah's military infrastructure". This could not be independently verified.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day seven of US-Israel attacks?
item.description: [Al Jazeera] After six days of US-Israeli attacks on Iran, the conflict is escalating as regional tensions rise.

item.title: Saudi Arabia downs drones near Riyadh
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Saudi Arabia downs drones near Riyadh

Saudi Arabia says its air defence systems have shot down three drones east of the capital, Riyadh, as the Israeli-US war on Iran continues affect regional countries.

The Saudi Ministry of Defence said the drones were intercepted on Friday.

"Three drones were intercepted and destroyed east of the Riyadh region," a defence ministry spokesperson said.

Earlier in the day, Saudi authorities also reported intercepting three missiles heading towards Prince Sultan Air Base near Riyadh, which hosts US troops.

item.title: Forcibly displaced Lebanese seek food, clothing, and comfort in shelters
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Fouad George is one of many helping their fellow Lebanese citizens, forcibly displaced by Israel’s military campaign.

item.title: Asia struggles to find fuel oil as Middle East exports plummet, sources say
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Jeslyn Lerh

SINGAPORE, March 6 (Reuters) - Fuel oil traders in Asia are struggling to secure alternative supply as the Iran war curtails shipments from key Middle Eastern suppliers through the Strait of Hormuz, prompting them to look to the West for replacement cargoes.

item.title: Iranians tell ToI they praise Israel, US for Khamenei’s end, but fear what’s next
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Speaking via activist, residents thank Netanyahu and Trump, recounting street celebrations after ayatollah killed, while describing unease over Iran's future amid ongoing bombardment

The post Iranians tell ToI they praise Israel, US for Khamenei’s end, but fear what’s next appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Indonesia says will withdraw from Board of Peace if it does not benefit Palestinians
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

JAKARTA, March 6 (Reuters) - Indonesia's President Prabowo Subianto has reassured local Islamic groups that he will withdraw from U.S. President Donald Trump's 'Board of Peace' if the platform does not benefit Palestinians, a government statement said on Friday.

The participation of Indonesia, the world's largest Muslim-majority nation, on the board, and its decision to contribute troops for a Gaza stabilisation force,has drawn criticism from experts and Muslim groups at home, who say it compromises its long-standing support for the Palestinian cause.

item.title: Unicef says reports indicate 180 children killed in Iran
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Unicef says reports indicate 180 children killed in Iran

The UN children’s agency says reports indicate that nearly 180 children have been killed in Iran since the conflict escalated.

Unicef said the figures also suggest many more children have been wounded during the violence.

"We urgently call on all parties to abide by their obligations under international law and ensure the protection of civilians."

item.title: Iran targets Israeli embassy in Bahrain, Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Multiple Gulf nations, Arab states, as well as Turkiye and Azerbaijan are caught in the crosshairs of the war.

item.title: Video: Smotrich says Lebanon's Dahiyeh 'will look like Khan Younis'
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Video: Smotrich says Lebanon's Dahiyeh 'will look like Khan Younis'

Israel’s finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, has said that Beirut’s southern suburbs, known as Dahiyeh, “will look like Khan Younis,” after the military instructed tens of thousands of residents to leave immediately or risk being attacked. 

“Dahiyeh will look like Khan Younis.”

Israel’s finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, has said that Beirut’s southern suburbs, known as Dahiyeh, “will look like Khan Younis,” after the military instructed tens of thousands of residents to leave immediately or risk being attacked pic.twitter.com/NpzlCWPiQn

— Middle East Eye (@MiddleEastEye) March 6, 2026

item.title: Residential areas hit in latest US-Israeli strikes on Tehran
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Residential areas hit in latest US-Israeli strikes on Tehran 

Residential areas in Tehran were struck in the latest wave of US and Israeli attacks early Friday, according to Iranian media reports.

Press TV said several neighbourhoods in the capital were hit, and footage showed a follow-up strike occurring as a reporter was covering the aftermath of an earlier attack.

Another strike reportedly hit near Tehran University, one of Iran’s leading educational institutions, according to a video shared by local outlet Mamlekate.

item.title: Mideast war enters 'next phase' as strikes hit Iran, Lebanon
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

Fresh strikes rocked Iran and Lebanon on Friday, as Israel vowed to escalate to a new phase in the Middle East war that has spiralled rapidly throughout the region and beyond.

AFPTV images from Beirut's southern suburbs showed mangled buildings and burned-out vehicles after heavy Israeli bombing overnight, with tens of thousands fleeing the destruction.

The war, now entering its seventh day, has dragged in global powers, upended the world's energy and transport sectors, and brought chaos to even usually peaceful areas of the volatile region.

item.title: Satellite images show damage at Iranian military, nuclear sites after strikes
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

Satellite images show damage at Iranian military, nuclear sites after strikes

Satellite images released by Vantor and Planet Labs show extensive damage at several Iranian military and strategic sites following recent strikes the US and Israel.

The images show damage at the Garmdarah missile base in northern Iran, buildings at the Natanz nuclear facility in Isfahan province, Iran’s Air Force headquarters in Tehran and the military harbour at Bandar Abbas along the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: GOP senator helps forcibly remove protester shouting ‘no one wants to fight for Israel’
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

US Capitol Police say demonstrator, a Green Party candidate in North Carolina, 'put everyone in a dangerous position by violently resisting and fighting our attempts to remove him'

The post GOP senator helps forcibly remove protester shouting ‘no one wants to fight for Israel’ appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: IRGC says Iran ready for prolonged war, threatens ‘painful blows’ with new weaponry
item.description:
[Middle East Eye]

IRGC says Iran ready for prolonged war, threatens ‘painful blows’ with new weaponry

Iran is prepared to fight a prolonged war and plans to introduce advanced weapons not yet used in the conflict, a spokesperson for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said on Friday.

Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naeini warned that Iran’s enemies should expect “painful blows” in the next wave of attacks.

Naeini said new Iranian technologies and weapons are "on the way" and have "not yet been deployed on a large scale".

He added that Iran is better prepared than during last year’s 12-day conflict with the United States and Israel, describing the current confrontation as a “sacred and legitimate war.

item.title: Trump urges Iranian Kurds to attack Iran as war widens
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Parisa Hafezi and Steve Holland

DUBAI/WASHINGTON, March 6 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump encouraged Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq to launch attacks against Iran as the Middle East conflict widened, with Azerbaijan warning it would retaliate for being targeted by Iranian missiles.

Israel on Friday said it had started a "broad-scale" wave of attacks against infrastructure targets in Tehran, as Gulf cities came under renewed bombardment by Iran.

item.title: Gulf carriers resume limited flights, but missile fire fuels uncertainty
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Julie Zhu

HONG KONG, March 6 (Reuters) - Emirates and Etihad Airways were resuming limited flight schedules to key global cities from their United Arab Emirates hubs on Friday, though the ongoing threat of missile fire piled pressure on airlines as they scramble to accommodate travellers.

With most airspace in the Middle East still closed over missile and drone concerns since the start of the U.S.-Israel war against Iran, authorities have been arranging charter flights and securing seats on limited commercial services to evacuate tens of thousands of people.

item.title: Exclusive-Trump on rising gas prices during Iran operation: 'If they rise, they rise'
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Steve Holland, Jarrett Renshaw, Nandita Bose and Bo Erickson

WASHINGTON, March 5 (Reuters) - President Donald Trump said on Thursday he was not concerned about rising U.S. gas prices driven by the widening Iran conflict, telling Reuters in an exclusive interview that the U.S. military operation was his priority.

"I don't have any concern about it," he said, when asked about the higher prices at the pump. "They'll drop very rapidly when this is over, and if they rise, they rise, but this is far more important than having gasoline prices go up a little bit."

item.title: US House joins Senate to vote down war powers resolution
item.description: [Al Jazeera] The US House of Representatives has joined the Senate in killing a war powers resolution for the war in Iran.

item.title: US probe said to indicate American forces likely launched strike that hit Iran girls school
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Officials stress US military investigators haven't yet reached final conclusion or completed investigation, which Hegseth confirms: 'We, of course, never target civilian targets'

The post US probe said to indicate American forces likely launched strike that hit Iran girls school appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: US, Israel say Iran missile fire down, most launchers taken out as offensive ramps up
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Hegseth says US firepower to surge as B-2s start dropping heavy bombs on missile sites deep underground; Zamir says new phase will target regime's foundations; cluster warhead fired at central Israel in late night attack

The post US, Israel say Iran missile fire down, most launchers taken out as offensive ramps up appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Hezbollah warns Israeli residents to evacuate towns near border
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

March 6 (Reuters) - Lebanon's Hezbollah warned Israeli residents to evacuate towns within 5 km (3.11 miles) of the border between the countries in a message posted on its Telegram channel in Hebrew early on Friday.

Hezbollah's message came less than a day after Israel warned residents to leave Beirut's southern suburbs, prompting an exodus from a swathe of the capital known as Dahiyeh.

Lebanon was pulled into the war in the Middle East on Monday, when Hezbollah opened fire, sparking Israeli airstrikes focused on Beirut's southern suburbs and on southern and eastern Lebanon.

item.title: Exclusive-US investigation points to likely US responsibility in Iran school strike, sources say
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

By Phil Stewart and Idrees Ali

March 5 (Reuters) - U.S. military investigators believe it is likely that U.S. forces were responsible for an apparent strike on an Iranian girls' school that killed scores of children on Saturday but have not yet reached a final conclusion or completed their investigation, two U.S. officials told Reuters.

Reuters was unable to determine more details about the investigation, including what evidence contributed to the tentative assessment, what type of munition was used, who was responsible or why the U.S. might have struck the school.

item.title: In shadow of war, Israel’s Winter Paralympian is ready to ski – but not on Shabbat
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Sheina Vaspi to skip two events at 2026 Milano Cortina Games due to Shabbat observance, but vows to ensure Israeli flag flies at Paralympic Games

The post In shadow of war, Israel’s Winter Paralympian is ready to ski – but not on Shabbat appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: In safety precaution, Israel bars Friday Ramadan prayers at Temple Mount amid Iran war
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Israeli authorities stress that all holy sites in Jerusalem's Old City are closed to worshippers, as gatherings remain capped at 50 people despite easing of restrictions

The post In safety precaution, Israel bars Friday Ramadan prayers at Temple Mount amid Iran war appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Three Australians were aboard US submarine that sank Iranian ship, PM Albanese says
item.description:
[Al-Monitor]

(Fixes typo in headline)

SYDNEY, March 6 (Reuters) - Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said on Friday that three Australian defence personnel were on board a United States submarine that sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in the Indian Ocean, but stressed they did not take part in the attack.

The strike, which occured off the southern coast of Sri Lanka this week, marked the first time since World War Two that the United States has sunk an enemy vessel with a torpedo. Sri Lankan authorities recovered the bodies of 87 sailors.

item.title: Video shows projectile exploding near Ali al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Videos showed a projectile striking near Kuwait's Ali al-Salem Air Base, which has hosted a number of US forces.

item.title: Novelty no more, Team Israel goes to bat for local game with return to World Baseball Classic
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Built mainly around American Jewish players, the team returning to the global tourney this week has become a symbol of Jewish identity and a driver for the sport's growth at home

The post Novelty no more, Team Israel goes to bat for local game with return to World Baseball Classic appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: How US sinking of Iranian warship blew hole in Modi’s ‘guardian’ claims
item.description: [Al Jazeera] India sees itself a security provider in the Indian Ocean. On Wednesday, it couldn't save even its own guest.

item.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi, the IRGC’s new commander?
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Ahmad Vahidi takes command of Iran's Revolutionary Guards as the country battles a devastating US-Israeli assault.

item.title: Despite war, Israel releases ‘Michelle,’ its 2026 Eurovision song
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Song to be performed by Noam Bettan at contest in Vienna this May is largely in Hebrew and French, with one verse in English, and 'touches on the whole spectrum of emotions'

The post Despite war, Israel releases ‘Michelle,’ its 2026 Eurovision song appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: US says Iran missile attacks down 90% after strikes from B-2 bombers
item.description: [Al Jazeera] The US says B-2 bombers have dropped dozens of 2,000-pound bombs on buried Iranian ballistic missile launchers.

item.title: Anti-Zionist activists stage blood libel display near White House
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Actors dressed as Netanyahu, Trump and Epstein dismember and devour fake dead baby, rehashing trope that has caused mass violence against Jews for centuries

The post Anti-Zionist activists stage blood libel display near White House appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Iran war live: Military academy among locations hit by US, Israeli attacks
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says 'no reason why we should negotiate', calls Washington untrustworthy.

item.title: IDF hits Hezbollah targets in Beirut overnight as group taunts Israel with evacuation order
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

Fresh Iranian missile salvo fired at northern Israel after 11-hour lull * UAE’s Etihad Airways resumes limited flight schedule, including to Tel Aviv * Four arrested in London on suspicion of spying on Jewish community for Iran

The post IDF hits Hezbollah targets in Beirut overnight as group taunts Israel with evacuation order appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Trump fires homeland security chief Kristi Noem, taps Oklahoma senator for job
item.description:
[Times of Israel]

US president, reportedly unhappy over Noem's performance at recent hearing over immigration crackdown, says 'MAGA warrior' Markwayne Mullin will take over; critics say more change needed at DHS

The post Trump fires homeland security chief Kristi Noem, taps Oklahoma senator for job appeared first on The Times of Israel.

item.title: Suspected Iranian missile debris ignites fires in central Israel
item.description: [Al Jazeera] Debris from an intercepted Iranian missile has landed near Petach Tikva in central Israel.

item.title: Trump offers immunity to IRGC, Iranian police who ‘lay down their arms’
item.description: [Al Jazeera] US President Donald Trump offered immunity to Iranian police and members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.
